title: Blackstonia serotina
taxonomy: Blackstonia serotina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a member of the genus Blackstonia, which is a small group of plants endemic to the Iberian Peninsula. The classification of Blackstonia serotina is further specified as Blackstonia serotina (Schrad.) Borbas. This classification indicates that the species was previously known as Schenkia serotina before being reclassified. The classification of Blackstonia serotina is widely accepted in botanical and horticultural contexts.

intro: Blackstonia serotina, also known as Blackstonia, is a plant species within the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Blackstonia serotina include iridoid glycosides, particularly gentiopicroside and secologanin, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Blackstonia is commonly prepared as a tincture or decoction, often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, and as a treatment for respiratory problems, including coughs and colds.
